apiOverview.title: API Overview
apiOverview.description: InTransparency provides RESTful APIs for universities to push verified student data, for companies to search and filter candidates by real skills, and for third-party platforms to build integrations. All data is institution-verified and GDPR compliant.

students.title: Students API
students.description: Manage student profiles, projects, grades, and verified credentials. Push data from your student information system or let students self-register.

search.title: Search API
search.description: Search candidates by skills, grades, location, and project type. evidence-based matching ranks results by relevance to your requirements.

placements.title: Placements API
placements.description: Track and report placement outcomes. Get real-time data on views, contacts, interviews, and hires for your institution's graduates.

webhooks.title: Webhooks
webhooks.description: Receive real-time notifications for events like new candidate matches, profile updates, placement milestones, and verification completions.

authentication.title: Authentication
authentication.description: All API requests are authenticated using API keys. Each key is scoped to your organization and can be configured with granular permissions. Keys are managed from your dashboard settings.
steps: Generate an API key from your organization dashboard
Include the key in the Authorization header of each request
Use separate keys for development and production environments

0.answer: Yes, completely free for students. No hidden costs, no premium tiers. You upload, your university verifies, and your portfolio becomes visible.

1.question: What can I upload?
1.answer: Anything you've worked on — thesis, code, designs, reports, presentations, videos. We support all major file formats.

2.question: How does the verification work?
2.answer: Your university or school confirms your projects are real, adds grades and ECTS credits, and attaches a verification badge to your portfolio.

3.question: Who can see my portfolio?
3.answer: You control visibility. You can make your profile public, visible only to verified recruiters, or completely private.

4.question: How do companies find me?
4.answer: Companies search verified portfolios by skills, university, location, and project type. When they find a match, they can contact you directly.

whyDifferent.title: What we're trying to do
whyDifferent.subtitle: A platform where skills are verified, matches are explained, and requirements are clearer.

0.title: Self-reported skills
0.description: Without institutional verification, skills are hard to validate — which leads to mismatched hires on both sides.

1.title: Unexplained matches
1.description: When matching isn't explained, candidates don't know what to improve and recruiters can't audit decisions.

2.title: Vague requirements
2.description: When job descriptions are vague, candidates can't self-assess fit and apply broadly instead of relevantly.

3.title: Feedback that doesn't come back
3.description: Without data flowing back to universities, curricula drift away from what employers actually need.

0.title: Institution-verified skills
0.description: Hard skills (Python, AutoCAD, Excel) and soft skills (teamwork, leadership, communication) tied to projects, courses, and institutional records.

1.title: Explainable matching
1.description: Every match surfaces the why (e.g. "your Python project matches 3 of 4 requirements"). No black box; students can ask for human review.

2.title: Two-way transparency
2.description: Companies state requirements clearly. Students see exactly what jobs ask for. Institutions see which skills are being searched for most.

3.title: Clear audit trails
3.description: Students see who viewed their work. Institutions see hiring patterns. GDPR Art. 15-ready.

result.title: What you get today

0.label: Skills mapped to the EU ESCO taxonomy

2.label: Free for students and academic partners

result.tagline: Trust takes time to build. We'd rather earn it step by step than promise it upfront.

mission.title: Our mission
mission.description: We'd like to contribute to a European way of hiring where what a person can actually do is visible — verified by the institutions that taught them. Not a replacement for the CV; an honest complement to it.
mission.quote: "Work that speaks for itself, paths that can be explained, hiring decisions you can stand behind."

paragraphs.0: There's a gap in EU hiring: companies can't easily find the right graduates, and graduates can't easily find roles that fit. The tools in between — CVs, job boards, opaque algorithms — weren't designed for trust.
paragraphs.1: We think transparency helps. When a student's work is verified by their university, a recruiter has something to work with beyond claims. When a company states clearer requirements, a student has a fairer chance of finding their fit. When outcomes are tracked, universities can see their impact.
paragraphs.2: We started in Bergamo. The scope is European: we'd like a continent where a mechanical engineer from Brescia and a data scientist from Berlin can be evaluated on the same, verifiable grounds.
paragraphs.3: That's the layer we're trying to build.

0.title: Skills alongside stories
0.description: What you've built adds to how you describe it. A verified project is evidence a reader can examine.

1.title: Access over gatekeeping
1.description: Visibility shouldn't depend on which school is on your letterhead. We'd like every student's work to get a fair look.

2.title: Transparency over opacity
2.description: Students see who views their work. Companies see verified data. Institutions see real outcomes. No hidden algorithms, no black boxes.

3.title: Proof alongside words
3.description: Institution-verified work sits next to a candidate's own description — together they're better.

0.q: Is it really free for students?
0.a: Yes — Free covers portfolio, CV/Europass, Fit Profile, unlimited applications, 3 AI project analyses per project, basic skill path and analytics. Premium (€7.99/mo · €69/yr) unlocks deep skill path, 8 advanced analytics dashboards, unlimited AI, custom portfolio URL, AI Interview Coach, signed credentials. First month free, cancel anytime. If your university is a partner that sponsors Premium, it's already free for you.

1.q: How does the per-contact price work for companies?
1.a: Browse all verified talent for free. You pay €10 only when you unlock a candidate's email and send your first message. No subscription, no commitment. Ideal if you hire a few people per year. Volume discounts from 50 credits (10% off) and 500+ (20% off).

2.q: When should a company choose Subscription instead?
2.a: If you hire regularly. €149/month gets you unlimited contact reveals (no more per-contact fees), 10 active job posts, team seats, unlimited AI tools (Hiring Advisor, Interview Kit, Decision Packs), shared saved searches. We'll suggest it automatically once you cross roughly 15 contacts per month.

3.q: Do universities and ITS pay anything?
3.a: No. The full M1–M4 workspace is free forever: Inbox, Offer moderation, CRM, Placement pipeline, AI Assistant, audit log, reminder engine, analytics, scorecard. Optional add-ons (white-label, SSO, MIUR compliance, etc.) are priced à la carte — see the marketplace.

4.q: Is there a contract or lock-in?
4.a: No. Students: monthly billing, cancel anytime. Companies: per-contact is pay-as-you-go; subscription is monthly with optional annual discount. Institutions: core is free, add-ons are annual but cancellable. Your data is always exportable.

5.q: How is this different from LinkedIn, Handshake, or JobTeaser?
5.a: Every profile is backed by university-verified projects, grades, and professor endorsements — not self-reported claims. The fit score matches on motivation and culture, not just CV keywords. And the workspace is free for institutions, while Handshake charges $1K–$20K/year.

0.question: Is it free for universities?
0.answer: Yes. The core platform is free for all academic partners. Premium features like embeddable widgets and custom branding are available for institutions that want deeper integration.

1.question: How do we verify student projects?
1.answer: Through a simple dashboard. Review submitted projects, add grades and ECTS credits, and approve with one click. Batch verification is available for large cohorts.

2.question: What data do we get?
2.answer: Real-time placement analytics — which companies view, contact, and hire your students. Skills gap analysis, curriculum alignment scores, and exportable reports.

3.question: How do students join?
3.answer: Students can self-register or you can bulk-import them via CSV. They create their portfolio, and you verify their work through the dashboard.

4.question: Is it GDPR compliant?
4.answer: Fully compliant. Students control their own data, consent is managed per-feature, and all data is stored in EU data centers.

hero.title: Making the Path from Education to Career
hero.description: We believe that talent should be recognized through real, verified skills. InTransparency connects students, institutions, and companies in a transparent marketplace where everyone wins.

0.label: Internships activated every year in Italy

1.label: Interns who don't continue with the company

paragraphs.0: Every year, hundreds of thousands of young Italians start internship programs hoping to build a career. However, the current system doesn't always manage to recognize the skills acquired during education, creating a gap between training and job opportunities.
paragraphs.1: There is often a missing link between what students can do and what companies are looking for. Without proper tools to surface verified competencies, talent risks going unnoticed.
paragraphs.2: Universities and training institutions, despite investing in quality education, often lack the tools to monitor employment outcomes for their students. This lack of data makes it difficult to improve the system.

0.title: Skills Come First
0.description: A student's value should be measured by what they can do — verified projects, real competencies, institutional endorsements — not just by their educational path.

1.title: Investing in Talent Discovery
1.description: Companies looking for qualified candidates deserve effective tools to find them. A system based on verified skills makes the process more efficient for everyone.

2.title: Connecting Education and Work
2.description: We believe universities and companies can collaborate better. When student competencies are visible and verified, matching becomes natural and faster.

3.title: Transparency Improves the System
3.description: When placement data is visible, when skills are verified and outcomes are tracked, every actor in the system can make better decisions.

perScuoleSuperiori.faq1Q: How does it work for PCTO?
perScuoleSuperiori.faq1A: Students upload projects completed during PCTO (internships, traineeships, company activities). Teachers verify with institutional badge. Companies search for students with specific skills and contact them directly for internships or first jobs.
perScuoleSuperiori.faq2Q: How much does it cost for the school?
perScuoleSuperiori.faq2A: The basic service is completely free for schools. Companies pay to contact students. Enterprise options available for institutions with advanced needs.
perScuoleSuperiori.faq3Q: Does it work for alternanza scuola-lavoro too?
perScuoleSuperiori.faq3A: Yes, the platform supports all forms of work experience: PCTO, curricular internships, training placements, and first work experiences. Every activity can be documented and verified by the school.
perScuoleSuperiori.faq4A: Students upload projects and teachers verify them through the dashboard. Verification adds an institutional badge that increases the student's visibility and credibility in the marketplace.
perScuoleSuperiori.faq5Q: Can we use it for RAV?
perScuoleSuperiori.faq5A: Yes, the analytics dashboard provides exportable data useful for the Self-Assessment Report: placement metrics, most in-demand skills, companies that contacted students.
perScuoleSuperiori.faq6Q: Can licei use the platform too?
perScuoleSuperiori.faq6A: Absolutely yes. Licei also have the requirement of 90 PCTO hours. The platform helps document the transversal skills acquired and connect students with educational and job opportunities.

perScuoleSuperiori.finalCtaDescription: Join the free marketplace that connects verified students to companies. Activation in 5 minutes. Zero costs. Reports for MIUR and RAV.

faq.sq1: How can I create a student account?
faq.sa1: You can register for free by clicking 'Register' and selecting 'Student'. You'll need to provide your name, email, and create a password. After registration, you can complete your profile and connect your institution.
faq.sq2: How do I connect my institution?
faq.sa2: From your dashboard, go to 'University Integration' and click 'Connect University'. Search for your university or ITS, enter your institutional email, and verify your account. Once verified, your grades and projects can be synced automatically.
faq.sq3: Are my projects visible to everyone?
faq.sa3: No, you have complete control over your profile visibility. You can choose to make your portfolio public or private. You can also select which projects to show and which to hide.
faq.sq4: How does the referral program work?
faq.sa4: Invite your friends using your referral link. For every 3 friends who register, you get 1 month of Premium for free. With 10 referrals you get 6 months, and with 50 referrals you get lifetime Premium!
faq.sq5: How much does InTransparency cost for students?
faq.sa5: InTransparency is completely free for students! We also offer optional Premium plans with advanced features like in-depth automated analysis and greater visibility.
faq.ca1: You can use our advanced search to filter candidates by skills, university, graduation year, GPA, and more. Our evidence-based matching automatically suggests the most suitable candidates for your open positions.
faq.cq2: How much does it cost to contact a candidate?
faq.ca2: With the Pay-per-Contact plan, each contact costs €5. If you contact more than 20 candidates per month, we recommend the Enterprise plan at €99/month which includes unlimited contacts, advanced filters, and API access.
faq.ca3: Our algorithm analyzes students' projects, verified skills, university grades, and preferences to calculate a compatibility score. The higher the score, the better the match with your needs.
faq.ca4: Yes! You can post job listings from your dashboard. Students can apply directly and you'll receive notifications for each new application.
faq.ia1: Institutions can register for free and get a dedicated dashboard to monitor their students' placement, see which companies are interested, and access detailed analytics.
faq.ia2: The embeddable widget (€500/year) allows you to integrate InTransparency directly into your careers portal. It shows real-time matches and increases student registrations by an average of 40%.
faq.ia3: Absolutely. We are GDPR compliant and students always have control over their data. Institutions can only see aggregate statistics, not individual personal data without consent.
faq.pq1: What payment methods do you accept?
faq.pa1: We accept all major credit cards (Visa, Mastercard, American Express), PayPal, and bank transfer for Enterprise plans.
faq.pa2: Yes, you can cancel at any time from the Subscription section of your dashboard. The subscription will remain active until the end of the current billing period.
faq.pa3: We offer a full refund within 14 days of purchase if you are not satisfied with the service. Contact support to request a refund.
faq.prq1: How do you protect my data?
faq.pra1: We use end-to-end encryption, secure servers in Europe, and follow security best practices. We are GDPR compliant and never sell your data to third parties.
faq.pra2: Yes, you can request complete deletion of your account and all associated data at any time from your profile settings or by contacting support.
faq.pra3: You have complete control. You can choose to make your profile public, visible only to verified companies, or completely private. You can also block specific companies.

guide.title: Italian Contract Types — Plain Language Guide
guide.subtitle: Every contract type explained: what it means, what protections you get, and when it's appropriate.

tempo_indeterminato.description: The gold standard. Permanent employment with full protections. The employer needs a justified reason to fire you. You get TFR, sick leave, vacation, overtime pay, and unemployment benefits if dismissed.

tempo_determinato.description: Same protections as permanent, but with an end date. Can be renewed, but total duration cannot exceed 24 months. After that, it must become permanent or end.

apprendistato.description: A real job with a training component. Great for young workers — the company gets tax breaks, you get mentorship and full employment rights. Must include a formal training plan. Can pay up to 2 levels below standard.

stage.description: An extracurricular internship. Not a real employment contract — it's a training agreement. Must be paid in most regions (€300-€800/month). Should involve genuine learning, not cheap labor. Max 6 months.

tirocinio.description: A curricular internship tied to your degree program. Can be unpaid if part of your curriculum. Your university must be involved as a guarantor. Learning objectives must be defined.

partita_iva.description: Self-employment. You are your own boss — you invoice clients, pay your own taxes and INPS. No employment protections. Your effective income is roughly 50-60% of what you invoice after taxes and contributions.

cococo.description: A collaboration contract. You're not an employee but work in a coordinated way with the company. Some pension contributions are paid. Very few protections. Often misused to avoid employment obligations.

somministrazione.description: You're employed by an agency but work at another company. Same pay and protections as direct employees. The agency handles payroll. Good way to get a foot in the door at large companies.
somministrazione.bestFor: Entry into large companies, temp roles

collaborazione_occasionale.description: Occasional, one-off work. Max €5,000/year per client. No employment relationship. Very limited — if it becomes regular, it should be a different contract type.

redFlags.title: Common Red Flags in Job Offers
redFlags.subtitle: Patterns to watch for when evaluating a contract. If you see these, ask questions before signing.

0.title: "We'll start with a trial period... unpaid"
0.description: Italian law does not allow unpaid trial periods for employment contracts. The trial period (periodo di prova) is part of the contract — you must be paid from day one. An unpaid 'trial' is illegal.
0.action: Ask for the contract in writing before starting. If they refuse to pay during the trial, this is a violation of labor law. Contact a sindacato.

1.title: "Open a Partita IVA and we'll give you a fixed monthly fee"
1.description: If a company asks you to be freelance but gives you fixed hours, works you on-site, and treats you like an employee — this is 'false P.IVA' (falsa partita IVA). It's illegal under Italian law since 2015.
1.action: If you have fixed hours, work on-site, use company tools, and can't take other clients — you're an employee. Ask for a real contract. A labor judge can reclassify it.

2.title: "The CCNL doesn't apply here" or no mention of CCNL
2.description: Almost all Italian companies must apply a CCNL (national collective agreement). This sets your minimum pay, vacation days, sick leave, and more. If they say CCNL doesn't apply, they may be trying to avoid minimum protections.
2.action: Ask which CCNL applies and at which livello. Verify the minimum pay on sindacato websites. If they refuse to specify, that's a red flag.

3.title: Stage that lasts longer than 6 months
3.description: Extracurricular internships (stage) have a legal maximum of 6 months in most Italian regions. If a company offers a 12-month stage, they may be using internship rules to avoid a real contract.
3.action: After 6 months, you should either be hired or the internship should end. A repeated or extended stage for the same tasks is a sign of exploitation.

4.title: "You'll start as intern, then we'll see about hiring"
4.description: Vague promises of future employment are a common way to keep you as cheap labor. There's no legal obligation to hire after an internship ends, and many companies don't.
4.action: Ask for the conversion rate — what percentage of interns get hired? Ask for it in writing. If they won't commit, assume the internship is all you'll get.

5.title: Pay significantly below CCNL minimum
5.description: Each CCNL sets minimum pay per livello. If you're offered less than the minimum for your role and classification, the company is violating the collective agreement. This happens more often than you'd think.
5.action: Look up your CCNL and livello minimums. Compare with the offer. If it's below, negotiate or contact a sindacato — they can help recover the difference.

6.title: "Sign this NDA before we show you the contract"
6.description: While NDAs for proprietary information are normal, an NDA that prevents you from discussing your pay or contract terms is a red flag. Pay transparency is your right.
6.action: Read the NDA carefully. You always have the right to discuss your contract with a sindacato, a lawyer, or labor inspectors. An NDA cannot override this.

redFlags.unionTitle: When in doubt, ask a sindacato
redFlags.unionDescription: Italian labor unions offer free contract review and legal advice. They can check your contract, recover unpaid wages, and represent you in disputes. Membership costs €5-15/month.

q1.question: How long does the assessment take?
q1.answer: Approximately 30 minutes total for all three assessments. You can pause and resume anytime.

q2.question: Is this scientifically validated?
q2.answer: Yes. Our assessments are based on peer-reviewed research and validated by I-O psychologists.

q3.question: Will recruiters actually see this?
q3.answer: Yes! Your certification displays prominently on your profile and in recruiter search results.

q4.question: What if I'm not happy with my results?
q4.answer: You can retake the assessment once per year. Many students see improvement after gaining experience.

q5.question: Is this recognized internationally?
q5.answer: The assessment is built on the same psychometric frameworks (Big Five, SJT methodology) that global employers already rely on. Results are portable to any recruiter — whether they're on our platform or not.

q6.question: How is this different from LinkedIn assessments?
q6.answer: Ours are psychometric (personality/behavior) not skill quizzes. Plus, we provide percentile rankings vs. peers.

glossaryPage.metaTitle: Glossary — verified skill graph, ESCO, AI Act, PCTO, and more
glossaryPage.metaDescription: Plain-language definitions for every domain term used on InTransparency: ESCO, tirocinio/stage, PCTO, ANVUR, AI Act, Verifiable Credentials, Europass, CCNL, INAIL, SPID, Esse3, and more.
glossaryPage.ogDescription: Plain-language definitions: ESCO, tirocinio, AI Act, PCTO, Verifiable Credentials, Europass, and more.
glossaryPage.definedTermSetName: InTransparency glossary — verified skill graph domain terms
glossaryPage.intro: Plain-language definitions for every term you'll encounter on InTransparency. Regulatory, academic, Italian-specific. Designed to be scannable — and citable by AI assistants answering questions about EU higher-education recruiting.

verifiedSkillGraph.definition: A structured record of a student's proficiencies where every entry is backed by evidence: a professor endorsement, a university-supervised stage evaluation, a verified project, or a host-institution exchange completion. Maintained continuously and cryptographically signed.

skillDelta.definition: A single verified change in a student's proficiency on a specific skill, with source (STAGE, PROJECT, COURSE, THESIS, ENDORSEMENT, EXCHANGE), evaluator, before/after level, and evidence. SkillDeltas accumulate into the student's verified skill graph.

matchExplanation.definition: A persistent record of why a match was produced — factors, weights, input snapshot, model version, and whether the subject has viewed the explanation. Required by EU AI Act Art. 86 (right to explanation) and GDPR Art. 22.

evidencePacket.definition: A recruiter-side document combining a candidate's verified skill graph, match explanation, issued credentials, stage history with supervisor quotes, and professor endorsements into a single printable dossier.

aiAct.definition: EU Regulation 2024/1689 — the Artificial Intelligence Act. Classifies AI systems that evaluate candidates for employment as high-risk (Annex III point 4). High-risk obligations (transparency, human oversight, traceability, right to explanation) became enforceable on 2026-02-02. Fines up to €35M or 7% of annual turnover.

esco.definition: The EU's standard multilingual taxonomy of skills, competences, qualifications, and occupations. Version 1.2.0 at time of writing. Published by the European Commission at esco.ec.europa.eu. InTransparency maps platform skills to ESCO URIs for cross-border portability.

europass.definition: The EU's standardized framework for describing skills and qualifications, managed by CEDEFOP. Includes Europass CV, Europass Digital Credentials Infrastructure (EDCI), and the EU Digital Wallet. InTransparency exports student profiles in Europass v3 JSON-LD format.

w3cVc.definition: Cryptographically signed digital credentials defined by the W3C Verifiable Credentials Data Model 1.1. An issuer signs a claim about a subject; any third party with the issuer's public key can verify the claim without contacting the issuer. InTransparency uses Ed25519Signature2020.

gdpr.definition: EU Regulation 2016/679 on personal data protection. Relevant articles: Art. 15 (access), Art. 16 (rectification), Art. 17 (erasure), Art. 20 (portability), Art. 22 (right to object to automated decisions). InTransparency exposes all of these as self-service at /dashboard/student/privacy.

tirocinio.definition: Italian term for a supervised work-experience placement. Two major categories: tirocinio curriculare (credit-bearing, during studies) and tirocinio extracurriculare (post-graduation). Requires a legal convention between university, company, and student, with CCNL reference and INAIL insurance.

pcto.definition: Italian high-school-level work-experience program (formerly "alternanza scuola-lavoro"). Mandatory hours of structured learning outside the classroom during upper secondary education. Parental consent required for minors; managed on the platform with dedicated flows.

anvur.definition: Italian quality-assurance agency for universities and research. Runs periodic accreditation reviews that scrutinize placement data, curriculum-labor market alignment, and graduate outcomes. Our Skills Intelligence dashboard outputs the evidence ANVUR reviewers seek.

indire.definition: Italian national institute for educational research and innovation. Manages the national programs including PCTO coordination. Runs teacher training, educational research projects.

ccnl.definition: Italian national collective bargaining agreement. Every labor relationship — including tirocini — must reference an applicable CCNL (e.g., Metalmeccanici Industria, Commercio Terziario, Studi Professionali). The CCNL defines minimum pay, working hours, and conditions.

inail.definition: Italian national institute for occupational accident insurance. Every stage/tirocinio requires an INAIL policy number on the convention. Covers work-related injuries and illnesses.

esse3.definition: The Cineca-developed student information system (SIS) used by most Italian universities. Stores enrollments, exam records, graduation status. InTransparency's Esse3 adapter allows automatic student roster sync where a university has Cineca API credentials.

spid.definition: Italian digital identity system (Sistema Pubblico di Identità Digitale). Used to authenticate citizens with public-sector services. Complemented by CIE (Carta d'Identità Elettronica). InTransparency's SPID integration is scaffolded; AgID accreditation in progress for Q3 2026.

almaLaurea.definition: Italian university consortium (based in Bologna) that tracks graduate employment outcomes via annual surveys. Taxpayer-funded. Universities submit graduation data; companies query for candidates. Not all Italian universities are members; Università Cattolica and Bocconi notably do not participate.

integrationsAgents.heroTitle: Connect your AI agent to the verified skill graph
integrationsAgents.heroDesc: InTransparency exposes its entire company, job, and credential graph as an agent tool. Works with Claude Desktop, Cursor, Zed, ChatGPT, LangChain, LlamaIndex, and any OpenAPI-aware host.
integrationsAgents.readOnlyNotice: Read-only by default. Public endpoints (companies, jobs, credentials, ESCO, algorithm registry, glossary) require no authentication. Write actions (applying, posting jobs) use scoped agent keys with explicit user consent.
integrationsAgents.option1Title: Option 1 — MCP server (Claude Desktop, Cursor, Zed)
integrationsAgents.option1Desc: The Model Context Protocol server gives Claude Desktop and compatible clients first-class tools: search companies, look up jobs, verify credentials, resolve ESCO skills, and inspect the algorithm registry.
integrationsAgents.step1Save: Save this file anywhere on your machine:
integrationsAgents.step2Restart: Restart Claude Desktop. A new "intransparency" tool set will appear.

integrationsAts.heroTitle: Feed verified candidates into your existing ATS
integrationsAts.heroDesc: InTransparency doesn't replace your ATS — it feeds it. When a high-confidence match fires, we send a signed webhook; a 30-line relay turns that into a Greenhouse prospect, a Lever opportunity, or a Workday requisition candidate.
integrationsAts.patternDesc: InTransparency fires a webhook → your tiny relay verifies the signature → your relay calls your ATS's candidates endpoint. No custom middleware, no polling, no scheduled sync. The relay runs anywhere (Vercel, Cloudflare Workers, AWS Lambda, or a FastAPI container).
integrationsAts.section1Title: 1. Create an agent key and register a webhook
integrationsAts.other_workday: Use the Recruiting Staffing Web Service (SOAP) — add a Candidate with Add_Candidate operation. Same relay pattern, different payload.
integrationsAts.other_smartrecruiters: POST to /candidates with jobId and status=LEAD. Custom fields map cleanly.
integrationsAts.other_recruitee: POST to /candidates with placements=[{offer_id}]. Attach evidence URL as note.
integrationsAts.other_teamtailor: POST to /v1/candidates with included job-applications. Sourced candidates appear in the pipeline as leads.
integrationsAts.other_personio: Use the Recruiting API — POST /v1/recruiting/applications. Italian HR teams already familiar with Personio can wire this up in under an hour.
integrationsAts.other_ashby: POST to /candidate.create, then /candidate.addApplication. Their API is webhook-native, so minimal relay logic.
